code.text: Alcohol use disorder, moderate — in early sustained remission

note.text: Patient meets 5 of 11 DSM-5 criteria: drinking in larger amounts or for longer than intended, persistent desire or unsuccessful efforts to cut down, craving, continued use despite recurrent interpersonal problems, and tolerance.

Updated to early remission at IOP completion visit. Patient achieved sustained sobriety (sobriety date April 14, 2023), completed 12-week IOP (~36 group sessions, 12 individual counseling sessions), AUDIT score improved from 28 to 4, liver enzymes fully normalized (AST 24, ALT 28, GGT 38 — all normal). Continue naltrexone indefinitely.

Telephone aftercare follow-up. Patient reports sustained sobriety for 3 months (sober since mid-April 2023), regular AA attendance (2-3 meetings/week), stable mood, and no naltrexone side effects. Updated clinical status to early sustained remission. Reinforced relapse prevention strategies. Advised establishing addiction medicine or psychiatry follow-up at new location due to planned geographic relocation. Care at Bridgetown Recovery Center concluded.
